creator: mi@FreeBSD.org
text:
Created attachment 210907
Bundle multiple pixel-widths

> The "promised behavior" was never correct. It implied 8 bit build,
> along with optional 10bit and 12bit.

The promise was correct -- we just never delivered on it. The attached version always builds the 8bit version, plus 10- and/or 12-bit versions if requested.

The libhdr10plus is also always built now. When debugging is enabled, nasm is invoked with "-g -O0" as well.

Things yet to be determined:

 1. On my machine the test executable still crashes as discussed earlier.
 2. Should the highest bit-width be the default -- to match the current state of affairs?
 3. Should it be possible to build ONLY one or both high-bit versions -- without the 8-bit variant?
